open_questions: Location, travel plan, and access instructions are unknown.
Dietary restrictions and allergies are unknown.
Final artichoke format is unresolved between breaded leaves and stuffed artichokes.
Whether the frozen fruit element is optional or locked is unknown.
The correct portion size for six guests is unresolved.
Whether the guest group typically eats average portions or clears all food is unknown.
Whether pasta should be made raw earlier or closer to service is unresolved.
Documents, equipment plan, packing list, and arrival targets are not specified in the notes.

known_truths: There is enough menu and prep detail to build a realistic menu, components, and prep chain.
Guest count is concrete enough for scaling and service rehearsal.
The dinner clearly requires make-ahead work plus day-of finishing.
why_this_case_matters: It tests whether ChefFlow can connect menu planning, prep, travel, service, and readiness without relying on clean inquiry data.
It stresses operational detail instead of only menu ideation.
It exposes whether service simulation stays truthful when the notes contain real ambiguity.
